questions.text: Explain the relationship between execution environments and decision environments in AAP 2.6. How are they similar and how do they differ?

rubric.correctness: Both are container images. Execution environments run playbooks via automation controller. Decision environments run rulebook activations via EDA controller.
rubric.completeness: Should mention: ansible-builder for EEs, container registries for storage, that both replaced Python virtual environments
key_facts: Execution environments are container images for running Ansible content (playbooks, roles)
Decision environments are container images for running EDA rulebook activations
Both are built and stored in container registries like private automation hub
Execution environments are built with ansible-builder
Both replaced Python virtual environments as the isolation mechanism

questions.text: What is the platform gateway in AAP 2.6 and what problem does it solve?

rubric.correctness: Platform gateway provides a unified web interface and single entry point for all AAP components (controller, hub, EDA). Introduced in 2.5.
rubric.completeness: Should mention: unified UI, single authentication entry point, replaces separate component logins
key_facts: Platform gateway provides a unified web interface for all AAP components
Single sign-on entry point replacing separate logins for controller, hub, EDA
Introduced in AAP 2.5
Handles authentication and routing to component services

questions.text: Describe how RBAC works in AAP 2.6. What are the key role levels and how do permissions cascade?

rubric.correctness: RBAC with roles at platform, organization, and object levels. System auditor is read-only. Permissions cascade from organizations to teams to users.
rubric.completeness: Should cover: system admin, system auditor, organization admin, team roles, object-level permissions
key_facts: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
System-level roles: admin and auditor
Organization-level roles: admin, member
Object-level permissions on projects, inventories, templates
Teams group users and inherit permissions
Auditor role provides system-wide read-only access

questions.text: What is Configuration as Code (CaC) in AAP 2.6 and how is it different from writing playbooks?

rubric.correctness: CaC manages automation controller settings (organizations, projects, templates, credentials) through declarative config files, not managing target hosts like playbooks do.
rubric.completeness: Should distinguish: CaC configures the platform itself, playbooks configure managed hosts
key_facts: CaC manages automation controller configuration declaratively
Defines organizations, projects, job templates, credentials as code
Different from playbooks which configure managed infrastructure
Enables reproducible controller setups and GitOps workflows

questions.text: Explain how content signing works in private automation hub. Why is it important?

rubric.correctness: Collections can be signed with GPG keys in automation hub. Signatures verify authenticity and integrity. Ansible Galaxy CLI can verify signatures.
rubric.completeness: Should cover: signing keys, signature verification, supply chain security
key_facts: Collections are signed with GPG keys
Hub administrators configure signing keys
Signatures verify collection authenticity and integrity
Protects against supply chain attacks and tampering
ansible-galaxy CLI can verify signatures on install

questions.text: If you need to add an execution node behind a firewall that cannot accept incoming connections, what mesh topology changes are required and what configuration is needed?

rubric.correctness: The execution node must peer TO the control/hop node (outbound connection). The peering direction must be set so the firewalled node initiates. A hop node may be needed as intermediary.
rubric.completeness: Should cover: peers variable, receptor_listener_port, peering direction, that the node declaring peers initiates the connection
key_facts: The node that declares peers in inventory initiates the connection
Firewalled execution node should declare the control/hop node as its peer
receptor_listener_port (default 27199) must be open on the receiving end
A hop node can relay traffic without executing jobs
Mesh topology is defined in the installer inventory file

questions.text: Walk through the complete process of migrating from AAP 2.4 RPM-based deployment to AAP 2.6 containerized deployment. What are the key steps and what data must be preserved?

rubric.correctness: Must upgrade version AND change installation type. Supported path is 2.4->2.6 or 2.4->2.5->2.6. EDA database must be dropped. RPM installer still works in 2.6 for migration.
rubric.completeness: Should cover: backup, inventory file changes, database migration, EDA database incompatibility, post-migration validation
key_facts: Supported upgrade path: 2.4 or 2.5 to 2.6
Migration = change installation type, upgrade = change version
RPM installer deprecated in 2.5, supported in 2.6 for migrations, removed in 2.7
EDA 2.4 database must be dropped (incompatible migrations)
Backup controller database before migration
Containerized installer uses different inventory format

questions.text: How would you set up an AAP deployment with high availability for the automation controller? What components need redundancy?

rubric.correctness: Multiple control/hybrid nodes behind a load balancer. External PostgreSQL with replication. Shared storage for hub if applicable.
rubric.completeness: Should cover: multiple controller nodes, load balancer, external database, mesh topology considerations
key_facts: Multiple control or hybrid nodes for controller HA
Load balancer in front of controller nodes
External PostgreSQL database with its own HA setup
Automation hub requires ReadWriteMany (RWX) storage for HA
Mesh topology should have multiple paths for resilience

questions.text: Explain how authenticator maps work when integrating AAP with an external LDAP directory. How do LDAP groups get mapped to AAP teams and organizations?

rubric.correctness: Authenticator maps define rules that map external identities to AAP roles/teams/organizations. Map types include: allow, organization, team. LDAP groups can be mapped to AAP teams.
rubric.completeness: Should cover: map types, trigger conditions, LDAP group to team mapping
key_facts: Authenticator maps are rules mapping external auth identities to AAP
Map types: allow (permits login), organization (assigns org), team (assigns team)
Maps have triggers based on attributes from the external provider
LDAP groups can be mapped to AAP teams via authenticator maps
Multiple maps can be chained with ordering/priority

questions.text: A job template is failing with 'No execution environment found'. Walk through the troubleshooting steps and explain the execution environment resolution order.

rubric.correctness: EE resolution: job template EE > project default EE > organization default EE > global default EE. Check that the EE image is accessible from execution nodes.
rubric.completeness: Should cover: resolution hierarchy, container registry access, pulling images, credential configuration
key_facts: EE resolution order: job template, project, organization, global default
Verify the EE image exists in the configured container registry
Execution nodes must be able to pull the image
Registry credentials may be needed for private registries
Check that the EE is assigned at the appropriate level

questions.text: What is the exact command to run the AAP containerized installer and what key files does it require?

rubric.correctness: The containerized installer runs via ansible-playbook with a specific inventory file. Key files: inventory, ansible.cfg.
rubric.completeness: Should mention the inventory file format, required variables, and any prerequisites
key_facts: Containerized installer uses ansible-playbook
Requires an inventory file with host groups and variables
Runs on RHEL 9.2+ with Podman
installer collection from automation hub or setup bundle

questions.text: What is the exact command to build a custom execution environment with ansible-builder? What files are needed?

rubric.correctness: ansible-builder build with an execution environment definition file (execution-environment.yml). The definition specifies base image, collections, Python packages, system packages.
rubric.completeness: Should cover: the build command, EE definition file format, key sections
key_facts: ansible-builder build command
execution-environment.yml definition file
Specifies base image (EE_BASE_IMAGE)
Can include collections via requirements.yml
Can include Python packages via requirements.txt
Can include system packages via bindep.txt

questions.text: What commands and configuration are needed to set up content signing in private automation hub?

rubric.correctness: Configure a GPG key in the hub. Sign collections through the hub UI or API. Configure signing service.
rubric.completeness: Should cover: GPG key generation/import, signing service configuration, signing workflow
key_facts: GPG key pair needed
Signing service configured in automation hub
Collections signed after approval
ansible-galaxy verify for client-side verification

questions.text: What is the exact command to run the AAP RPM-based installer and what are the key inventory file variables?

rubric.correctness: ./setup.sh from the installer directory. Key variables: automationcontroller_*, automationhub_*, automationedacontroller_* in the inventory file.
rubric.completeness: Should cover: setup.sh, inventory file location, key variable groups
key_facts: ./setup.sh is the installer command
Inventory file defines host groups and variables
Key variable prefixes: automationcontroller_, automationhub_, automationedacontroller_
admin_password and pg_password are critical variables
Host groups: [automationcontroller], [automationhub], [automationedacontroller], [database]

questions.text: How do you configure the receptor listener port and verify mesh connectivity between nodes?

rubric.correctness: receptor_listener_port (default 27199) set in installer inventory. Verify with receptorctl status or the mesh visualizer in the controller UI.
rubric.completeness: Should cover: port configuration, firewall rules, connectivity testing
key_facts: receptor_listener_port variable (default 27199)
Set in installer inventory file per node
Firewall must allow the port between peered nodes
receptorctl status for CLI verification
Mesh visualizer in automation controller UI shows topology
